idioms.definition: Sometimes, but not regularly or often
examples: I eat junk food once in a while.
Once in a while, she visits her grandmother.
It's fine to treat yourself once in a while.
idioms.usageTip: Use this when something happens occasionally but not regularly. It's interchangeable with 'from time to time'.

idioms.definition: Doing something quickly because you don't have much time; rushing
examples: Sorry, I can't stop to talk – I'm in a hurry.
She left the house in a hurry and forgot her keys.
He wrote the report in a hurry and made several mistakes.
idioms.usageTip: Use 'in a hurry' to explain why you can't do something or why something was done quickly and perhaps carelessly.

idioms.definition: Right now; currently; at this particular point in time
examples: I'm busy at the moment – can I call you back?
At the moment, prices are rising quite fast.
She's not available at the moment.
idioms.usageTip: Very common in spoken and written English. Use it instead of 'right now' for a slightly more formal tone.

idioms.definition: At the scheduled or expected time; not late
examples: The train arrived on time for once.
Please make sure you submit your assignment on time.
She's never on time for anything – she's always ten minutes late.
idioms.usageTip: Don't confuse 'on time' (punctual) with 'in time' (before a deadline or before something happens).

idioms.definition: Occasionally; at irregular intervals; not very often
examples: From time to time, I like to go for a long walk in the countryside.
He still calls me from time to time.
From time to time, the system needs to be restarted.
idioms.usageTip: Slightly more formal than 'once in a while'. Both mean the same thing and can be used interchangeably.

idioms.definition: For now; for the present moment, though things may change in the future
examples: For the time being, we'll have to manage without a new assistant.
Let's leave the plan as it is for the time being.
She's staying with her parents for the time being while she looks for a flat.
idioms.usageTip: Use this to signal that a situation is temporary and will likely change later.

fillInExercises.sentence: I only see my old school friends ___ – maybe once or twice a year.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Once in a while' means occasionally, not regularly.

fillInExercises.sentence: Sorry, I can't stop to chat – I'm ___.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'In a hurry' means doing something quickly because you don't have much time.

fillInExercises.sentence: ___, the company needs to cut costs, so no one is getting a pay rise.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'For the time being' means for now, with the implication that things may change later.

fillInExercises.sentence: The bus was ___ today, which was a pleasant surprise.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'On time' means arriving or happening at the scheduled time.

fillInExercises.sentence: I'm not available ___ – I'll call you back in about an hour.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'At the moment' means right now, currently.

fillInExercises.sentence: ___, I enjoy a quiet evening at home instead of going out.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'From time to time' means occasionally, at irregular intervals.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Hey Tom, are you coming to the team meeting?

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: I'll be there – but I'm [in a hurry|in a hurry] right now. I need to grab some coffee first.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Don't be late! The manager gets really annoyed when people aren't [on time|on time].

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: I know, I know. I'll be there in five minutes. Is the whole team there [at the moment|at the moment]?

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Most of them. We can start without you [for the time being|for the time being], but please hurry.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: I'll be quick. I only do this [once in a while|once in a while] – I'm usually very punctual.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Sure you are! I'll save you a seat.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Thanks, see you in a second!

lines.speaker: Mia
lines.text: I haven't seen you in ages! How have you been?

lines.speaker: Josh
lines.text: Really well, thanks. Life's been hectic [at the moment|at the moment], but in a good way.

lines.speaker: Mia
lines.text: We should meet up more often. We only see each other [from time to time|from time to time].

lines.speaker: Josh
lines.text: I know. Things are crazy with the new job [for the time being|for the time being], but it should calm down soon.

lines.speaker: Mia
lines.text: Fair enough. Do you still go to that Italian restaurant near the station?

lines.speaker: Josh
lines.text: Yes, [once in a while|once in a while]. Their pasta is amazing.

lines.speaker: Mia
lines.text: We should go together sometime. Are you free this weekend?

lines.speaker: Josh
lines.text: Let me check – I'll message you later today. I can't think right now, I'm [in a hurry|in a hurry] to get to an appointment.

quizExercises.instructions: Match each idiom with its correct meaning.

pairs.meaning: rushing because of lack of time
at the scheduled time, not late

quizExercises.sentence: The train arrives ___ – exactly at 9:00 AM every day.
quizExercises.explanation: 'On time' means at the scheduled or agreed time.

quizExercises.sentence: We don't have a permanent solution yet, so this arrangement will have to do ___.
quizExercises.explanation: 'For the time being' signals a temporary arrangement that may change in the future.

quizExercises.question: In which situation would you use 'once in a while'?
options: When you do something every single day without exception
When you do something occasionally, not regularly
When you are rushing to get somewhere
quizExercises.explanation: 'Once in a while' describes something that happens occasionally, not on a regular basis.

quizExercises.question: Which sentence uses 'for the time being' correctly?
options: She finished her homework for the time being and went to sleep.
He arrived for the time being, so we could start the presentation.
We'll use this room for the time being until the new office is ready.
quizExercises.explanation: 'For the time being' means for now, implying the situation is temporary.
